findings.title: Formatting-coupled assertion makes the close-surface regression test brittle
findings.rationale: The assertion requires the exact substring "- `$close`\n\n## Public Skill Responsibilities", coupling the test to Markdown adjacency and section ordering rather than the intended contract. Non-semantic edits (wrapping, inserted context lines, or list reordering that still includes `$close`) can produce false failures and block proof gates without a real contract regression.
findings.suggested_next_action: Replace adjacency-sensitive substring matching with resilient checks that independently validate required contract atoms (for example: public list contains `$close`, responsibilities section contains `$close` ownership text), while avoiding dependence on exact blank-line/layout formatting.

findings.title: Runtime public-surface guard misses explicit `$review-loop` vs legacy `$review` doc contract
findings.rationale: The test proves filesystem removal of `.codex/skills/review/SKILL.md`, but it does not assert that runtime docs explicitly keep `$review-loop` in the public skill list while excluding legacy `$review`. Because the receipt claims this naming repair, the current proof can pass even if docs later reintroduce stale `$review` wording or drop `$review-loop` from the list.
findings.suggested_next_action: Add explicit runtime-doc assertions for both presence of `- `$review-loop`` and absence of a legacy `- `$review`` public-surface bullet (or equivalent tokenized check) so the receipt claim is directly regression-guarded.
